title: Columns governed by the attributes _.Automated_, _.Description_, _.Index_, _.Official_, _.Stars_ for `--format`, not exhibited when the `--list-tags` is set
body:
### Issue Description

Hello. Not exhibited when the `--list-tags` is set:
- columns governed by the attributes _.Automated_, _.Description_, _.Index_, _.Official_, _.Stars_ for `--format`.
- characters in between attributes.

Same observations be the image name **fully** (e.g. _docker.io/library/httpd_) or **partially** (e.g. _httpd_) specified.

For comparison, when the `--list-tags` is not set:
```
$ podman search -f=is-official --format "{{.Name}} | {{.Index}} | {{.Official}} | {{.Automated}} | {{.Description}} | {{.Stars}}" --limit 1 httpd
docker.io/library/httpd | docker.io | [OK] |  | The Apache HTTP Server Project | 4462
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

```
$ podman search --list-tags --format "{{.Name}} | {{.Tag}} | {{.Index}} | {{.Official}} | {{.Automated}} | {{.Description}} | {{.Stars}}" --limit 1 httpd
NAME                     TAG
docker.io/library/httpd  2
```


### Describe the results you received

Not exhibited:
- Columns governed by the attributes _.Automated_, _.Description_, _.Index_, _.Official_, _.Stars_.
- ` | `.

### Describe the results you expected

Exhibited:
- Columns governed by the attributes _.Automated_, _.Description_, _.Index_, _.Official_, _.Stars_.
- ` | `.

comments.body:
Thanks, @Ricky-Tigg!

I think that's the same issue as https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/19033, so I am closing this one.  Please speak up if I am mistaken.
I prefer to trust your judgement.
